-- 1 of 4 --

Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 5
!
Now we are talking about creating other types of prelaunch content pretty quickly
here.
!
There are lots of other types of prelaunch content in addition to video.
!
One of them is audio, but this is generally not recommended.
!
Back in the old days we used audio a lot. If you have a great audio and maybe you did
an audio interview with someone that would be great or you got a few different audio
interviews and you're going to put them together into one audio.
!
You can use that, but you want to turn it into screen capture video. Take that audio and
build in some slides for it, build a mind map for it, get some photos or pictures, and
marry up that audio to the screen capture and it will raise the perceived value in a huge
way.
!
Generally I'm not a big fan of audio these days, it's very rare for people to come to a
website, sit there and click a play button and listen to an audio. Turn it into a video and
it will really be a lot more impactful.
!
You can do a pdf reports.
!
You create a document, turn it into a pdf, which you can do with pretty much all kinds
of software these days. That feature is built right into most word processing software
pages for the Mac or Microsoft Word, very easy to create pdf.
!
You can do it as a written report where you write a short white paper type of document.
!
You can do create a process map, a mind map, a flow chart, an info graphic. The great
thing about pdfs is they have really high perceived value and people will opt in if you
tell them they can download a pdf, they will opt in for it. Really works very, very well.
!
Another thing that works great is mixing a pdf with a video. And I do that with my
Product Launch Blueprint. I'll have a video with me going through the actual pdf. The
other thing is, is it doesn't have to be super long. I've knocked out a great pdf report in
a matter of few hours. If you can do a pdf that's six to eight pages and then you put in
some graphics and a title page and a conclusion you can end up with a twelve,
fourteen, sixteen page report pretty darn quickly. Pdfs; are a very high perceived value.
It could be very, very effective especially when you match them with videos like I do. If
you put those two together, in terms of getting people to opt in to your prelaunch
content, that is a winning strategy.

-- 2 of 4 --

!
Now 	there 	are 	even 	more 	kinds 	of 	pre-launch 	content, 	such 	as 	webinars 	or
teleseminars.
!
They can be used during your pre-launch or they could be used after you open your
cart. I tend to use them more after I open my cart or right at the end of my pre-launch,
right before we are about to open a because a webinar means that they can see your
screen and you end up with a presentation that's not unlike a screen capture. The
difference with a webinar is you're delivering that live and people are all dialed in at
that one time for the webinar.
!
Teleseminar is purely via the telephone; they are only hearing it via audio.
!
A lot easier to create a teleseminar than a webinar because you don't have to bother
creating slides or having to worry about what it looks like, so teleseminars can be very
easy.
!
Freeconferencecalling.com; it's a place you can get what we call a bridge line. That's
how you actually do your teleseminar. You will dial into the service from your phone and
you've got a pin code that you type in and that makes you the moderator so you can
speak then other people can dial in. They enter a pin code as an attendee so they can
listen. Very, very simple to use; very easy to use. Freeconferencecalling.com you can
actually get a free, what we call bridge line that allows you to do that.
!
If you're going to do a webinar, you can use InstantTeleseminar or you can use
GoTowebinar, those are two services. GoTowebinar is generally more flexible, more
powerful and it costs a whole lot more money than InstantTeleseminar. I've used both
of them. They're both very nice services. And then you can also, as pre-launch, you can
give away software.
!
This is not something that I have done, but you can actually create software, probably
not the most super-expensive crazy software and you probably just want to use the
software as one piece of prelaunch content either for your first piece of prelaunch
content or your last piece of prelaunch content.
!
It is really high perceived value. It can be very, very effective. This is not something I've
done. I've helped out on a couple launches where they did it, but I haven't done this.
I'm not much of a software builder at this point in my life so I can't tell you a lot about
this from experience so you're really on your own with this one.
!
Like I said, I have seen this be very, very effective so if it ties in well with your launch,
just create some piece of software if you have experience or comfort doing that then
it's something to consider.
!

-- 3 of 4 --

Circling back, remember in terms of creative prelaunch content. It is all about horses
for courses.
!
What you use depends on your situation and what you can create and what your assets
are. Maybe you just did this amazing interview with some world class expert in your
market that just brings huge authority and social proof to your launch. Now you’ve got
this great audio you turn that into a screen capture video. There you go; boom you've
got it.
!
On the other hand maybe you're just really great on camera or you're really well-spoken
on camera, you've got someone that can shoot video for you. Then use a video. Maybe
you're just an incredible writer. You can create incredible reports, use a pdf. What you
use should be based on your situation and what your assets are and what you're
comfortable with.
!
Video is really great for lot of situations, and it's good for you to get comfortable with
video. Even if you're not comfortable right now, it's a great thing to start working on
because, like I said earlier in the first video about creating prelaunch content series,
people 	can 	connect 	with 	you. 	It's 	a 	lot 	easier 	to 	appear 	human 	and 	appear
approachable and personable and you've got a personality than it is for most people to
do in writing.
!
It's usually easier to do that on video. People can see you. They can sense your
passion, your sincerity so it's great for lots of situations. Even if you're not comfortable
on video now, if you just start shooting now, you'll be comfortable sooner rather than
later. It doesn't that long to get comfortable in front of the camera.
!
Remember, finally, it is about progress. It's not about perfection.
!
You will get better at this. Just go ahead and knock it out. Get some prelaunch content
done. If it's not perfect then you can always do it over again, but it's really about
progress and getting better over time. I've improved, I've seen so many of my PLF
owners improve over time. It's amazing how you just get better and better by taking
those baby steps and moving forward and increasing your skills a little bit at a time.
!
!
!
!
